title: Creamy Norwegian Smoked Salmon Pasta
description: A rich and creamy pasta dish featuring smoked salmon and a unique double-fry technique, perfect for satisfying midnight cravings.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the fettuccine pasta according to package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat and double-fry the smoked salmon pieces until crispy and golden brown.
Remove the smoked salmon from the skillet and set aside on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
In the same skillet, melt the unsalted butter over medium heat and whisk in the all-purpose flour to make a roux, cooking for 1-2 minutes.
Slowly pour in the heavy cream, whisking constantly to avoid lumps, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the cream sauce simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens and coats the back of a spoon.
Stir in the grated parmesan cheese until melted and smooth, then season the sauce with salt and black pepper to taste.
Add the cooked fettuccine pasta to the cream sauce and toss to combine, then stir in the chopped fresh dill and double-fried smoked salmon pieces.
Serve the creamy pasta hot, garnished with additional dill and parmesan cheese if desired.
